qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via unvalidated HTTP redirect following in _http_get
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via unvalidated HTTP redirect following in scanner.py:_http_get (CWE-918), which is a different vulnerability class and root cause from the advisory-described vulnerability (unsafe pickle deserialization allowing io.FileIO arbitrary file read and urllib.request.urlopen SSRF exfiltration). The advisory root cause is pickle deserialization with unsafe constructors (io.FileIO, urllib.request.urlopen), while the finding root cause is HTTP redirect validation in the HTTP client layer. The vulnerability classes, affected code paths, and attack mechanisms are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes unsafe pickle deserialization that allows io.FileIO for arbitrary file read and urllib.request.urlopen for SSRF-based data exfiltration. The only qualifying finding (ssrf-via-http-redirect) describes a different vulnerability: HTTP redirect following without validation in scanner.py:_http_get. These are distinct vulnerabilities with different root causes, affected code, and exploitation mechanisms.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-9726-w42j-3qjr describes pickle deserialization vulnerability using io.FileIO and urllib.request.urlopen to achieve arbitrary file read and SSRF exfiltration
The advisory root cause is unsafe pickle deserialization allowing dangerous globals (io.FileIO, urllib.request.urlopen) in pickle payloads
Finding ssrf-via-http-redirect describes SSRF via unvalidated HTTP 302 redirect following in scanner.py:_http_get function
The finding root cause is HTTP redirect validation in the HTTP client layer, not pickle deserialization
The finding PoC demonstrates redirect following from Server A to Server B, not pickle-based file read or exfiltration
The advisory references commit a01c58d5dd7960db557b849817c0ab83ab111ef1 and PR #55 for pickle blocklist improvements, while the finding targets HTTP client redirect handling
